amRdata 0.0.0.9000

    • intro

Run CD-HIT (via Docker) on concatenated protein FASTA and return output paths

Source: R/data_processing.R
runCDHIT.Rd

Concatenates protein FASTA files listed in the DuckDB table "files" (column faa_path), runs CD-HIT in a Docker container, and returns paths to the generated input and clustered output FASTA.

runCDHIT(
  duckdb_path,
  output_dir,
  output_prefix = "cdhit_out",
  identity = 0.9,
  word_length = 5,
  threads = 0,
  memory = 0,
  extra_args = c("-g", "1")
)

Arguments

duckdb_path

Character scalar. Path to the DuckDB database with a table named "files" containing at least the column faa_path with absolute or relative paths to protein FASTA files.

output_prefix

Character scalar. Prefix for output files. Defaults to "cdhit_out". The function will create:

  • <output_dir>/<output_prefix>_input.fa

  • <output_dir>/<output_prefix>_proteins

identity

Numeric. Sequence identity threshold for clustering (CD-HIT -c). Defaults to 0.9.

word_length

Integer. Word length parameter (CD-HIT -n). Defaults to 5. Must be compatible with identity according to CD-HIT constraints.

threads

Integer. Number of threads (CD-HIT -T). 0 uses all available cores. Defaults to 0.

memory

Integer. Memory limit in MB (CD-HIT -M). 0 means unlimited. Defaults to 0.

extra_args

Character vector. Extra CD-HIT flags appended at the end (e.g., c("-g","1") to use the most similar sequences as cluster representatives).

output_path

Character scalar. Directory where concatenated input and the clustered output FASTA will be written. Created if it does not exist.

Value

A list with:

cdhit_input_faa

Character scalar. Path to concatenated input FASTA.

clustered_faa

Character scalar. Path to clustered output FASTA (CD-HIT -o).

Details

The function:

  • Connects to a DuckDB database and reads the table "files".

  • Filters rows to ensure no "NA" string values and pulls faa_path.

  • Concatenates all *.faa files into a single input FASTA: <output_dir>/<output_prefix>_input.fa.

  • Runs CD-HIT via Docker with the specified parameters and writes clustered proteins to <output_dir>/<output_prefix>_proteins.

  • Verifies successful output and returns paths to input/output FASTA files.

Contents

Developed by Janani Ravi.
